entry.title: Re: How to trigger workflow in EC using API to the manager of a Contingent Worker to extend or not e
entry.content:
Very interesting!

@rahulsharma - Would it be possible to share more details on the configuration? Additional screenshots of the Configure Object Definition screen?

I would like even more knowledge on:
How the MDF is configured?
How the iFlow is configured?

entry.title: Re: MIGO error VLA312 for partial delivery
entry.content:
Hello

I faced this problem. 

The partial delivery not allowed is drived by the confirmation sequence of the Set up confirmation control.

The GR assignment needs to be unflagged in confirmation category LA to allow the partial MIGO against the quantity in the inbound delivery. 

Spro>MM>Purchasing>Confirmations>Set up confirmation control>Confirmation control key ( same as the confirmation control keys on the line item in Purchase order) > confirmation sequence> Gr assignment Flag or not accrding to the needs.

Hope it will helps for people in a similar situation

regards,

Ingrid

entry.title: Re: EML Create by association on existing Root
entry.content:
Hi,

How did you solve that??

I'm having the same problem:

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_billingdocumenttp

ENTITY billingdocument

EXECUTE createfromsddocument AUTO FILL CID

WITH VALUE #(

( %param = VALUE #( _reference = VALUE #( ( sddocument = <fs_outbound_delivery>-id-content

%control = VALUE #( sddocument = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ) ) ) )

 

RESULT DATA(lt_result)

FAILED DATA(ls_failed)

REPORTED DATA(ls_reported).

I«m getting failled 'UNSPECIFIC'

Thanks in advance,
Ines Martins

entry.title: TM - Output request - Sender Country Can't be Initial
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When I attempt to view the waybill or any other output in the TM app "Manage freight order" under the tab “Output request” tab and try to preview the output, I encounter the error message: “Sender country/region can’t be initial.” I’ve already filled in the “Sender Country” field in the “Define Rules for determination of Master form templates,” but the issue persists. Additionally, I’ve updated the business partner, but the error remains. Do you have any other solutions for resolving this error? 

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SAP Transportation Management

entry.title: IDOC FAILURE AT EWM SYSTEM
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have several idocs processed from ERP but are failing at EWM system. The idoc error is framed as- "No means of transport found for packaging material XXXXXXXX". If anyone has any idea on how to resolve this issue pls help urgently.

entry.title: Re: Enable Services for Object (GOS) in Quality Notificat...
entry.content:
Hi @StefanKonradi 

 

GOS is not enabled in S/4HANA Cloud for Quality Notifications . There is no customizing available to enable it 

Instead , we have the "Linked Documents" and Create Document option where you can upload attachments to Document Info Records .

 

I checked in our internal system and GOS is enabled for Document Info Records so you use that instead and then attached the corresponding DIR the Quality Notification

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: format date after deploy app
entry.content: why it matters? show me your coding. very likely your code issue.

entry.title: Re: Composites in SAP Analytics Cloud.
entry.content: Hi! Thanks for an interesting blog. We are utilizing composites as navigational components in our stories, however there is a strange behaviour, when a composite is dragged to multiple pages. So it appears multiple time in a story. it seems to lose the remote function connection somehow. For example, I import my navigation "NAVI" into the story, drag it into Page 1 and Page 2, rename these widgets to NAV1, NAV2. After the story is saved and reopened, all functions called for NAV1 (like NAV1.setFilters(); ) are marked red as "unused property" or "not a function", whereas the functions for NAV2 work just fine. Is that a bug? It seems we can not use it as a navigation component for multi-page stories right now... Thanks for the answer, Martin

entry.title: Re: Integrate B1 to third party WMS
entry.content: Did you find any sollution please?

entry.title: Customer Engagement apps / roles within SAP S/4 Utilites
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a S/4 Utilities on premise with the stand of late 2021, which already is supposed to contain the customer engagement apps and roles, so e.g. Agent Desktop UI5 app within the role S4C_UT_IC, at least as far I understood it from SAP Help: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/021b182b0c47416c8fafed67ebfd78a9/6bee35f4b26f4a248aa282d379a54976.html?locale=en-US&version=2021.latest

I wasnt able to find this role neither in the pfcg, nor in the SAP fiori apps reference library. Where can I find any technical information on running this on premise, e.g. prerequisites, required components etc? Why is this app not within the SAP library, it isnt supported any longer? I have searched for "Agent Desktop", maybe I have missed something?

I ran through diverse documents provided from SAP like migration guides, SAP Help documentation etc. and didnt found any answers. Im not interested in migrating BP's, contract data etc, I dont want to have any interaction with SAP cloud, all I wanna do is take a shallow look to corresponding fiori applications to have a rough idea if they are suitable to replace old good CIC. Thanks

entry.title: Re: What configuration needs to be done either in ECC(S4)...
entry.content: OK, what engine is that? ChatGPT?

entry.title: Where can I find enabling materials and gain the basic skills necessary for procurement management?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Managing SAP Ariba Procurement learning journey learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is a work in process and courses will be added to the journey as they are completed.
The Managing SAP Ariba Procurement learning journey is curated to elevate the proficiency of learners mastering the nuances of SAP Ariba Procurement management. This curriculum delves into the intricacies of purchasing strategies, contract compliance, catalog management, supplier engagement, and beyond. Learners will navigate the complexities of SAP Ariba with a focus on strategic optimization, efficiency enhancement, and driving significant value through procurement processes. Designed for those who are already familiar with the basics of SAP Ariba, this journey offers deep insights into its more sophisticated capabilities.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, administrators, and business users and consultants will be able to perform basic skills necessary for procurement management.

Prerequisites

  • A basic understanding of SAP Ariba Procurement. 
  • As well as Discovering SAP Ariba Procurement.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: Re: F4 Help for custom fields created using Custom Fields & Logic - InApp Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi Vijay,

Can In-App extensibility be used to add a drop down field with values from a custom table? This is for Create Purchase Requisition App - F1643.

Thanks,

Smitha

entry.title: Re: Interested for SIT Kolkata 2024 ?
entry.content: I filled up the form for SIT 2024 yesterday but haven't received the link for the MCQ test yet which is scheduled for tomorrow. Kindly tell us when will we receive it.

entry.title: BRF+; sales order confirmation on item level
entry.content: We are using BRF+ to submit the EDI sales order confirmation (ORDER_CONFIRMATION) and order changes (ORDER_CONFIRMATION_CHANGE). By standard this is a header message, but we want to have this on item level. Only comfirmed items should be in the message for standard items, and third party items should be included when the PO is created and confirmed. Every time a sales order item is confirmed, a new message should be triggered containing all confirmed items. So far, we could not find a way to do this using BRF+. Has anyone a solution to this issue?

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Changes to the Free Tier Option for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content: Hi @SergioFerrariIt , the Custom Code Migration app is part of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment and is also impected by this change.

entry.title: Re: Testing SAP Build Process Automation task UI in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
@MioYasutake 

Northwind is getting the same CORS error. 

 

Yes, I'm doing a GET call in the controller. I was struggling to get the Odata call to work using the normal configuration. I assumed the Taskwrapper solution was creating some issues, so I went with an ajax call in the controller.

entry.title: Re: Using SAP CAP with IntelliJ
entry.content:
Hello Nils,

CAP CDS Language Support for IntelliJ is now publicly available at https://github.com/cap-js/cds-intellij for users of commercial IntelliJ IDEs including IDEA Ultimate and WebStorm. (Since the plugin builds upon JetBrains' LSP API, it is unavailable in IDEA Community.)

Feel free to download and install the IDE plugin from GitHub. And let us know in case anything seems missing or broken.

We'll update here once the plugin is available in JetBrains Marketplace (integrated in the IDE).

Best regards,
Tim

entry.title: Dynamic calendar view in SAC
entry.content:
Is there a way to get a dynamic view like the below

for a dynamic range too. I want the user to be able to arbitrarily select any two start and end dates from the data ideally in a calendar view.  The biggest use case is just last month, last quarter and last year but id like to have that option. Im having a hard time seeing such a functionality. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

stage building in TM is done for adv ICo stock transfers in the same manner as for other processes.
Stage building is done automatically (based on customizing of logistics integration profiles) and can be amended manually.
For adv ICo stock transfers freight costs are assigned to the respective company based on the intercompany Incoterms.

Best regards,
Bernd

How did you solve that?

Thanks,
Ines Martins

Unfortunately, have no knowledge who implemented it and it looks like custom software.
What do you think?
Best regards
Helene

entry.title: Error 403 when calling Process Automation API on deployed SAP Build App
entry.content:
Hi all,

currently i'm facing an issue, where I receive an error 403 on trying to start my Process Automation workflow with this API: https://api.sap.com/api/SPA_Workflow_Runtime/path/post_v1_workflow_instances.
It's working when I start it with Postman. On the preview-version from SAP Build Apps it is also working fine.

But on the deployed version it isn't working. 
I found out, that the bearer token is missing:

The common request header is filled. It looks like the data query "query.authConfig.authToken" is empty.

Does anybody has an idea what's wrong here? In the past it worked like a charm.

Thanks,
Vincent

entry.title: SAC BW Live - No automatic YoY or Previous Period or Variance Calculations when using 0CALMONTH ?
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

can somebody remind me what the prereqs are for having automated calculations like YoY etc. for a BW live model. Do I need 0CALDAY in it? Right now I just have 0CALMONTH and nowhere the auto calculations show up.

Is that on purpose? 

Thanks and best regards
Axel

Best regards
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Subject: Request for Free SAP Certification Exam Attempt

Dear David Chaviano,

I trust this message finds you well. I'm reaching out to request assistance in securing a complimentary attempt at the SAP certification exam. My name is Tam Phan, and I'm a third-year student delving into the world of ERP. I'm eager to augment my expertise and knowledge through obtaining the SAP certification.

As a woman, I'm committed to advancing my career within the technology sector. I firmly believe that obtaining the SAP certification will widen my career prospects and furnish me with the essential skills to thrive in this field.

Recognizing that exam registration may entail financial obligations, I'm seeking support to participate without financial constraints. I sincerely hope for your consideration and support in this matter.

Thank you for your attention to my request.

Warm regards,

Tam Phan

entry.title: Do we have any certification related to SAP build (covering build process automation)?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I can see that the citizen developer (LCNC) certification that was available before is not active at the moment. Do we have anything else available except C_WZADM_01?

entry.title: Re: First Half 2022 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Public Services?
entry.content:
@Farah_Gonzalez Hi Farah,

Do you have any update on having the WBS element as an additional cost object in the employee timesheet?

Many thanks,

Elena

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with Pricing Schemes in SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Hi Brinda ( @Brinda911 ),

technically, as of today it's still possible to also use the "legacy option" and store prices directly in Subscription Billing and not using the pricing schemes.

However, this is clearly not recommended. Especially when starting something new. From a functional perspective, pricing schemes cover all key scenarios.
Even if it looks like requirements can be covered without pricing schemes at first, there's quite some risk that requirements evolve and you may be excluded from using all the great features of the Price Calculation service.

The use of the Price Calcualtion service is available to all Subscription Billing customers and no additional charges apply.

Same recommendation can also be found in the product documentation, e.g. from
https://help.sap.com/docs/subscription-billing/feature-overview/pricing#pricing-features

Best regards
Stefan

entry.title: Re: Fleet Management SAP PM - two fuel cards
entry.content: Hi, What you mean by Fuel Cards ? You mean counters ?

entry.title: Recap - SAP ALM at SAP Insider Las Vegas 2024
entry.content:
Now that the dust has settled, I wanted to take the time to share with you some of my experiences and key topics from the SAP Insider Las Vegas 2024 event that took place from March 19-21.

This was the first time I have attended this well-organized event at the Bellagio Resort and Casino, and I was very impressed with the number of topics covered in over 150+ sessions which ranged from application development, integration, finance, cybersecurity, and more. The event was well-attended and there were many fantastic opportunities to network between sessions as well as in the exhibit hall during evening events.

As one of the product managers for SAP Cloud ALM, I was co-presenting with Tim Steuer, VP for ALM at SAP, at several sessions covering SAP Cloud ALM adoption, the transition process from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM, and how to use SAP Cloud ALM for Operations to effectively monitor SAP BTP.

The first order of business was to deliver the Monday pre-conference session “Best Practices to Approaching Transformation: Combine SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM and Tricentis for an Optimized Outcome” with Andreas Zehent, VP of Cloud Success Services for SAP Signavio.

Customers have been asking: How do SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, and SAP Cloud work together? What is the purpose of each offering, and when should they be used during my transformation project? What integrations are available today, and what is planned? The half-day pre-conference session aimed to answer these questions by introducing SAP's Transformation Suite approach that assists customers with their business transformation by combining the power of SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, and SAP Cloud ALM (with Tricentis Test Automation) into one cohesive methodology.

Attendees gained valuable knowledge about how the suite's components work collectively to provide an unmatched suite experience and guidance for initiating the journey to the cloud. Andreas presented and demoed the available integration between SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio, and Tim covered the future plans for integrating SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio with SAP Cloud ALM. Tim also revealed how SAP Cloud ALM (included in customer maintenance) is the anchor point for customer transformations, enables the latest innovations to be adopted at scale, and is also the key driver for customer Clean Core initiatives.

And for the first time, SAP presented a unified map of where each product adds value to the transformation journey and laid out the current and planned integration points, as shown below:

We received great feedback from the attendees, who told us that they were impressed with the level of detail as well as the logical approach of the session content.

For the “Move from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM: Tips and Tricks” session, Tim and I explained how SAP supports customers during their transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM with services, learning offerings, and additional tools. We introduced the resources and recommendations available at the Transition Center to guide customers through the process depending on their situation, and showed how the Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM helps customers determine their SAP Solution Manager usage and allows them to map out their transition strategy. In addition, tools to migrate SAP Solution Manager content like documents and test cases to SAP Cloud ALM are planned for later this summer, along with a methodology roadmap to help plan the transition project.

In the session “Re-Imagine Your Application Lifecycle Management Strategy” we laid out SAP’s ALM portfolio and how SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Focused Run helps customers coordinate innovation while keeping the business healthy. SAP Cloud ALM provides a simple and cloud-native solution for implementing and operating the intelligent enterprise, while SAP Focused Run (on the SAP pricelist) provides a powerful solution for handling large landscapes with advanced system management requirements.

Our session “Operations of SAP Business Technology Platform with SAP Cloud ALM” outlined how SAP Cloud ALM’s capabilities support different SaaS services as well as custom-built applications based on SAP Business Technology Platform. I demoed how SAP Cloud ALM acts as a single entry point to manage and monitor the health, integrations, exceptions, and performance of SAP BTP services and applications, and discussed how SAP Cloud ALM uses the open-source OpenTelemetry standard to instrument custom-built applications in the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Environment or SAP BTP, Kyma runtime.

However, it wasn’t a case of all work and no play. Tim and I were able to visit the Sphere Las Vegas, which was very impressive both inside and out.

We also took advantage of some liquid refreshment courtesy of the partners in the exhibit hall 😊

Overall, SAP Insider Las Vegas 2024 was a fantastic event, and we look forward to attending next year!

entry.title: Consume external OData as Batch Request
entry.content:
Dear ABAPers,

is there any way to bundle outgoing OData calls in a batch request (e.g. calling a non-SAP OData API from an ABAP report)? I could do this using the HTTP Client class and building up a batch body as string and send it via POST request, but this doesn't feel very nice 🙂

I did some research and couldn't find anything pointing that way (also looking at client proxies).

Many thanks in advance for some hints or confirmation, that there's no build-in functionality!

P.S.: Not talking about implementing a gateway OData with change sets.

entry.title: Re: MM/SD organizational structure with retail and partner stores
entry.content: I can understand 90 Store fronts as Plants. Why not WH are considered as Storage Locations?

entry.title: Why does the formula use tax codes which don't even exist in VAT declaration box?
entry.content:
For Germany localization, I am seeing A71 and V10, but some of these codes don't even exist! Are they hard-coded in SAP or am I missing some setup or something here?

I have since setup a new company with German localization and everything aligns. The only between between the two companies are, one has Elster addon enabled and another one does not have it enabled yet. Need to test further if Elster is amending the tax codes? Has anyone faced this issue with German localization with latest version of SAB B1 v10 (PL240)

entry.title: Re: SAP Console and S/4Hana
entry.content:
Hi David.

I confirm you that SAP Console works well with S/4 Hana

entry.title: Re: Substitutions in SAP FI
entry.content: Reymond, I have attached the details of the substitution which I have done

entry.title: The Procurement Monthly - March 2024
entry.content:
Summary

Some quality reports this month, with the release of 3 staple reports that I look forward to, namely the trade in transition, the digital procurement report and the Sustainable barometer. What’s interesting is the trends reports all lead quite nicely to the deeper dive reports (suggesting that they really are trends!!)

Some quick takeaways, whilst supplier diversification is still in vogue, there are some areas where supply base reduction is re-emerging to improve relationships.

AI is a thing, no, really it is everywhere as Ardent states.

Environmental concerns (Scope 3, carbon footprint) are taking priority as focus areas, maybe as more legislation is driving this.

Cost management is still (and realistically always will be) a priority for many reasons, from funding the current and future business to providing alignment opportunities for procurement.

I hope you enjoy the summary and links are provided for downloads.

Ardent Predictions & Trends

To start this month, we have a couple of papers that identify mega trends and predictions, the first of which is from Ardent Partners. Ill discuss more about the trends than the predictions, but both are good reads.

To pull out some interesting and connected trends, Savings remain a top priority, and cost of capital making procurement more important. When cost of capital is high, it costs more to run a business, hence the importance of savings remaining key, even though one of the other trends is that inflation has stabilised.

The geopolitical tension, and deglobalisation will make supply chains interesting, which may drive the increase of friend shoring or at least duplication of supply chains. Finally, the explosion of contingent/external workforce spend is important, and as out study with Art of Procurement identified has the potential to return great value to organisations. Clearly the war for talent not just in procurement but across the organisation, driven by new technology and the changing nature of “core” means that there are many avenues which lead to the increase in the use of external workers, however you define them.

AI of course is, as the paper identifies, everywhere!

Procurement Mega Trends – Efficio

Hot on the heels of the Ardent report came this article from Efficio, that looks at the mega trends for the future of procurement. To summarise the 4 areas:

  • Digital Disruption, AI, Collaborative platforms, and cyber security. The report projects that AI will be critical for organisations by 2025 (39% of respondents to a Statista survey as opposed to 11% in 2022).
  • The war for talent increases – both for attracting new, but also for retention.
  • Sustainability – 70% of c suite members state the ESG topics are important strategic priorities for their companies, however the spend profiles on where the companies are directing efforts doesn’t necessarily match.
  • Geopolitical disruption – Being agile in the face of disruptions, becomes the new normal for procurement.

PWC Digital Procurement Study

This is my read of the month!

The 5th edition of the PWC digital procurement survey, has increased the responses to around 1,000 making it one of the largest procurement studies (though not the largest. Wait for the Economist Impact – sponsored by SAP).

Key takeaways

The top 5 strategic priorities identified in the report are: Procurement performance (Read cost control) 65%, Digital transformation 46%, CSR – 35%, Sourcing 35%, SRM 32%. There is a drop off post this in terms of %.

So, we see this continuing theme as outlined in several other start of the year papers around the importance of digital transformation as organisations seek greater efficiency and more speed to their operations and this is confirmed in the drivers and benefits section where 70% identified that efficiency gains were a driver, and 62% identified that they had seen the benefits of these efficiency gains.

The report also identifies that user experience is emerging as a new driver of digital, which is something that we have identified in the Economist Impact study over the last few years.

The expectation is that the digitisation rate for the next 3 years will increase to around 70% by 2027, this is despite the actual rate being quite static between the last 2 reports (2022 – 4). Data analytics, and source to contract are the top 2 investment areas by 2027, although the overall set of use cases seem to be consolidating together. Risers of use cases include Contract Lifecycle Management, E invoicing, and Gen AI (shocking!!)

There are a couple of deep dive areas, ESG, for example showed that 59% of organisations prioritised environmental risks (E, as opposed to SG) with carbon footprint management being identified as one of the larger areas of concern.

There are great focusses both from a region and sector perspective that make for interesting reading as well.

AI In contracting – World Commerce & Contracting

One of the mega trends we identified earlier was AI everywhere, so having this report from World Commerce and contracting that looks into the use of AI in the contracting process is timely.

This study draws from over 500 responses globally and sees an increase in those planning to implement AI in the contracting process by 25% over the last 6 months. However, whilst this rise may seem impressive it still only represents 20% of responses, with those not planning to implement also increasing to 61%. What is interesting is this stands in opposition to the personal enthusiasm for AI which has more than doubled and stands at 80%

The top reasons given for this apparent disconnect are concerns of data privacy and security (57%), especially since that AI systems process large volumes of data, the quality of the output and general lack of trust in the data (46%) which is dependent on how the large language models are trained.

I did find interesting that the authors asked ChatGPT for what they believed the benefits would be, and whilst similar in ranking, the number 1 reason by practitioners was to analyse risk and compliance which was number 2 for ChatGPT. Number 1 for ChatGPT was freeing up time for more strategic work which was number 4 for the practitioners. The other disconnect was the assisting in contract negotiations which practitioners ranked second, whereas ChatGPT ranked 5th, this could be a negotiating position by ChatGPT though………..

Sustainable Procurement Barometer – Eco Vadis

Similar to AI, Sustainability made it into the megatrend’s reports, and I always welcome the Eco Vadis barometer reports and there is a connection from the digitisation importance from the PWC (and other) reports. Only 6-25% of sustainable procurement programs digitally integrate ESG into procurement processes. As the rate of digitisation increases, then you would hope this would increase accordingly, however I think that this is a critical piece for ESG to truly solidify into becoming business as usual that its digitally integrated. The most mature area in terms of digital integration is spend analytics, closely followed by ordering systems and catalogues. Manual integration is at 53% for sourcing and new supplier selection.

Top drivers for running a program include delivering on corporate goals (I personally read this as aligning to the business, something always at the top of procurements agenda), compliance with regulations and reducing risks (which could be complying to legislation) and building resilience. Focus areas include Net Zero and scope 3 emissions (like the PWC Report) and human rights/social practices.  

The report identifies certain practices which leading sustainable programs use at a greater rate than non-leaders, these include Risk and hotspot mapping, targeting both strategic and high-risk suppliers in activities such as self-declarations, use of 3rd party databases onsite audits and corrective action programs.

Trade In Transition – Economist Impact

This series starting in 2022, tracks how global trade and supply chains evolve in response to changing economic conditions. Its important to understand this, as the mega trends earlier identified that the geopolitical climates are driving some change.

Key findings from this report (for procurement) show that whilst supplier (and supply chain) diversification is still the most prevalent strategy for organisations, there is the early identification of the next wave of reconfiguration as organisations seek to balance, the increased in the number of suppliers with a concentration on creating better partnerships.

AI has become an indispensable tool for 98% of executives, impacting at least one aspect of their supply-chain operations. As the World CC report also identified reducing costs are a key expected benefit of AI and this report identifies that, 34% of companies are banking on AI to curtail overall trade expenses.

The favoured just-in-case models are being refined as companies strive to strike the right balance between building resilience and managing efficiency (costs).

In 2023, companies maintained 9.0 weeks of inventories, compared to 10.1 in 2022 and 8.9 in 2021. This signals a slight recalibration in 2023, likely due to the capital intensity behind higher inventories, and as we identified from the Ardent report earlier, the cost of capital is increasing.

In the face of geopolitical shocks, 'friend shoring' and the creation of parallel supply chains emerge as the go-to strategies for business resilience. Geopolitical shocks continually disrupt global trade, driving the restructuring of supply chains to centre stage.

Executive Perspectives – BCG

This BCG report looks at the c suite strategic priorities in 2024, and there are some important take aways for procurement and supply chain leaders to note.

A critical success factor for organisations to achieve success in 2024 is enduring cost management, i.e. building continuous improvement, organisation wide rather than the one and done cost reduction programs. This is viewed as the most critical strategic priority across all regions and industries.

Its all aspects of costs according to the report, direct and indirect procurement, labour and non-labour overhead, which will be firmly in focus. The report stresses though that its not the initial cost savings but the need to sustain these cost structures over time.

“There were cost reductions at the start, but the cost eventually crept back”.

This isn’t the rush for profit priority according to the report, but the need to incest further into specific programs for other priorities (in other words, procurement funds the organisational improvement programs), such as increased digitisation and AI, talent management, sustainability, future supply chains, and business expansion.

Everest –Procurement Transformation in the age of AI

We have mentioned several times in this and previous reports about the reemergence of digitalisation as a priority especially to access greater efficiency through AI. This report looks at how procurement transformation through the prism of AI and how AI will impact procurement transformations.

Discussing current levels of adoption, and AI use cases across source to contract, the report also compares AI capabilities with Gen AI capabilities. Of most interest to me was the framework identified for prioritising AI Implementation, using Impact and adoption as the 2 axes.

Impact includes, financial, operational and business. Adoption includes criticality, constraints and complexity.

I think overall this is a pretty good way to look at prioritisation of use cases. Overall, the top right quadrant (i.e. high adoption potential, and high impact, includes spend analytics, market intelligence guided buying support and supplier onboarding and ID. Which follows several AI use case reports I’ve reviewed previously. Obviously, each organisations impact and constraints will be different, but I think the concept is sound.

 

As always reach out to discuss more, links to the reports are included and always happy to hear your thoughts!

entry.title: Re: SAP ERP Functionality for EDI Processing: UoMs Determination for Inbound Orders
entry.content:
Thank you @M_Kalyabin . 

A good detailed document. 

I am currently working on Inbound SDPACK Message in SAP and the UoM PCE in Inbound Message is not converted to ST. When we send Outbound DESADV, ST is converted to PCE...

entry.title: How to created Org Structure for Retail with 7 Regional Warehouses and 3000 Retail Stores In MM?
entry.content: Does it make sense to assign 7 Regional Warehouses as Storage Locations and Retail Outlets(stores) as Plant

entry.title: SAP Ariba Integration scenarios with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Ariba solutions extend the core operational processes, providing full source-to-settle functionality with both S/4 HANA Private cloud and S/4 HANA Private cloud systems.

In this blog, we will see the Ariba technical scenarios which can integrate into S/4HANA Public cloud. Considering the restriction of leveraging the Ariba standard integration method, we will also see what integration method can be used to connect Ariba and S/4HANA Public cloud system.

S/4 HANA public cloud integration with SAP Ariba includes guided buying, collaborative sourcing, and networked-based, end-to-end supplier collaboration to deliver a new and simplified user experience and a seamlessly integrated working model with suppliers using SAP Business Network.

We can extend the core capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Ariba solutions in a single-instance deployment. This enables you to receive business documents from SAP Ariba solutions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and to execute end-to-end processes. Ariba FPC can not be integrated with S/4HANA public cloud.

Available Integration scenarios between Ariba and S/4HANA Public cloud system:

Below mentioned technical scenarios can be integrated from Ariba to S/4 HANA using SOAP APIs using Managed Gateways Passthrough integration. Integration method for below scenarios is different than integrating on-prem S/4 system, So no managed gateway add-on required to connect these systems

 

Sl #

Technical scenario number

SAP Ariba Technical Scenarios name

S/4HANA Public cloud Integration

1

 

Supplier Management (SLP) – Bi-directional sync

yes

2

4BL

Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing

yes

3

4AZ

Contract Management with SAP Ariba Contracts

yes

4

2NV

Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying

yes

5

42K

Source-to-pay with SAP Business Network (Commerce Automation)

yes

SAP S/4HANA public Cloud in connection with SAP Ariba applications allows you to largely benefit in streamlining your procurement processes:

1.   Integrating Supplier Data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Ariba Solutions Using SAP Master Data Integration

We can use SAP Master Data Integration (MDI) to replicate supplier data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Ariba solutions.

SAP MDI replicates supplier data to SAP Ariba applications through SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network. The following diagram illustrates this process:

Supplier data integration using SAP MDI supports the following Source to Pay business processes for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud integration solutions:

  • Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying (2NV)
  • Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing (4BL)
  • Contract Management with SAP Ariba Contracts (4AZ)

SAP Documentation link: About Supplier Data Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Using SAP Master Data Integration | SAP Help Portal

3087667 - Connecting from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Master Data Integration service - SAP for Me

Reference link for Bi-directional update:

SM-31650: Support for Bidirectional Integration of Supplier Data with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Using SAP Master Data Integration (MDI) | SAP Help Portal

2.   Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing

Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing (4BL) enables to identify sources of supply for purchase requisitions in SAP S/4HANA public Cloud, using the community of suppliers in SAP Business Network and the SAP Ariba Sourcing solution. Note that this scenario is the successor of Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing (1A0).

You can find more information about feature overview in the below SAP help documentation link: Sourcing with SAP Ariba Sourcing (4BL) | SAP Help Portal

3.   Contract Management with SAP Ariba Contracts

Contract Management with SAP Ariba Contracts" (scope item 4AZ) enables you to perform strategic sourcing activities and to integrate prices from SAP Ariba Contracts into operational systems, that is, SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

You can find more information about feature overview in the below SAP help documentation link: Contract Management with SAP Ariba Contracts (4AZ) | SAP Help Portal

4.   Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying

The guided buying capabilities of SAP Ariba are designed for employees who order goods using a self-service. Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying allows procurement organizations to scale self-service procurement across spend categories while enforcing compliance.

Note: Procure to pay with SAP Ariba Buying & Invoicing with S/4 HANA integration scenario is deprecated

For more detail about 2NV can be referred in the below link: Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying (2NV) | SAP Help Portal

5.   Automation of Source-to-Pay with SAP Business Network (42K)

The scope item Automation of Source-to-Pay with SAP Business Network (42K) enables you to seamlessly connect from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to your suppliers in SAP Business Network. You can send them requests for quotations, asking for price and quantity information as well as for shipping costs. You can also send electronic purchase orders and goods receipts to your suppliers using SAP Business Network, and you can receive electronic order confirmations for materials, advanced shipping notifications, and invoices in SAP S/4HANA Cloud from your suppliers. You can also send payment advices in electronic form to your suppliers in SAP Business Network. All data is routed through SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network.

SAP Documentation link : Automation of Source-to-Pay with SAP Business Network (42K) | SAP Help Portal

Hope this blog help you to understand in the possible scenarios can be integrated between Ariba and SAP S/4HANA public cloud.

entry.title: Re: SAP Console and S/4Hana
entry.content: Hi @mario_bisonti2 - thanks for confirming

entry.title: How to enhance the Request Material(MDG) App Lean App ID(F0502)
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Example Requirement: Enhance the Standard "Request Material(MDG)" app to have a Custom field and send to MDG backend staging table. From MDG the custom field will be mapped to standard Manufacture part number.

1) Front end: Is there any way to Enhance "Request Material(MDG)" via BAS and in BAS this app itself is not loading via adaptation project as the Fiori Library shows Enhancement points. In WEB IDE can we do extensions, we are using development tool BAS and how to get the license for webide full stack in BTP.

2) Backend: The Service Associated With the app is MDG_MATERIAL_SRV which should be extended with Z service can we use CFL instead of Append structure.

entry.title: Re: ABAP-Types with class
entry.content:
@jrgkraus I've unfortunately found that not to be true all the time. For variables typed by data element LVC_VALUE, the help_id of the cl_abap_elemdescr doesn't contain the domain name TEXT128, but instead contains LVC_VALUE again.

Do you know of any other alternative to get the domain through RTTI? I'm still on 7.56, on-prem.

entry.title: Connection restrictions and their relation to user groups in SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database
entry.content:
Connect restrictions are access policies and essentially enforcing the limited access to SAP HANA Database for users which belong to a specific user group*. For example, they can be used to restrict access for administrator users to a given IP range, or for technical users to specific applications. 

They enable customers the flexibility to limit access for all users or parts of their user base, for instance, to enable only administrators to log in from the customer's IP range and application range based on the application session variable. 

Benefits: 

  • Provides more fine-granular control over security configuration settings 
  • Allows customers to further secure their systems against attacks from the Internet 

More about connect restrictions you can read in SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database Security Guide: User Management -> User Groups -> Connect Restrictions. 

 

Setting up the connect restrictions by using SAP HANA Cloud Central 

For a demonstration of how you can make use of connect restrictions, I will use HANA tooling, and more specifically, SAP HANA Cloud Central**. 

A typical customer scenario that is pursued with the usage of this functionality is to limit the access of the users from specific applications they use to access HANA. Also, it is a case when technical users of HANA DB should not be allowed to connect or log into specific apps, with the help of which you can manipulate data or settings in HANA, like HDBSQL or HANA Cockpit/DB Explorer. 

To create connect restriction policies, you need to access SAP HANA Cloud -> section “User & Authorization Management” -> subsection “User groups”. 

If you cannot see this tile or access SAP HANA Cloud Central, please check SAP HANA Cloud Administration Guide documentation for needed tools subscription and missing authorizations for user. 

Creating a User Group-Specific Connect Restriction 

A single connect restriction can contain multiple applications and IP conditions.  

To configure those conditions, a user requires an OPERATOR object privilege on the given user group. This can be done by a creator of the given user group or another user who has GRANTOR privilege on this given user group. 

1. If not yet created, in the “User & Authorization Management” section, subsection “User groups,” set up a user group, for which you will subsequently create required restrictions. For us, it will be Administrators1. Move required users to this Group. 

2. Scroll down and create a connect restriction NetworkRestrictions1 with conditions for IP addresses in CIDR notation and application. The restriction allows connections for hdbsql application from the mentioned IP addresses and IP ranges. 

You can define multiple connect restrictions in a single statement. The following example shows how to define connect restrictions for IPS_V4, IPS_V6, and HDBSQL: 

3. The definition of connect restrictions and a list of connection restrictions by user group can also be viewed in the system view USERGROUP_CONNECT_RESTRICTIONS. 

An example for the USERGROUP_CONNECT_RESTRICTIONS table would be as follows in the SQL console of SAP HANA Cloud Central if you execute the steps above: 

Additional syntax for user group creation statements with parameters of connect restrictions can be found in SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL Reference Guide -> CREATE USERGROUP Statement (Access Control). 

Evaluation of Connect Restrictions 

After the user has successfully authenticated, connect restrictions are evaluated as part of the user authentication process. If the user group has connect restrictions, members of that group can only connect if at least one of the defined connect restrictions allows access. 

Note: A single connect restriction can consist of multiple conditions, and all must be met to allow access for the user.

Deactivating connection for all Users of a User Group 

Use the ENABLE | DISABLE CLIENT CONNECT option to control whether or not the users in a user group can connect to the SAP HANA database, for example, to stop users temporarily from connecting during updates or troubleshooting activities. 

Sample Code:  

ALTER USERGROUP MyUserGroup DISABLE CLIENT CONNECT 

If you disable client connects for a user group using this feature, no user of that user group can connect, even if connect restrictions would allow it. 

Validating a User Group-Specific Connect Restriction 

In the connect restriction bar on the top right, you also can validate whether a connect attempt would be possible given the active connect restrictions of a user group: 

The window will pop up where you can test different options and get the results: 

Also, you can use the table function PUBLIC.VALIDATE_USERGROUP_CONNECT_RESTRICTION to do that and use the table function PUBLIC.VALIDATE_USERGROUP_CONNECT_RESTRICTION_DETAILS to display details of each connect restriction if a login was allowed for a specific condition. 

Some things to keep in mind about validation views: 

  • This table functions validates all connect restrictions of a user group 
  • Returns a detailed result of each connect restriction if a login is allowed 
  • The check is done for the active and inactive restrictions of a user group: 
    • Client IP Address 
    • Application Name 
  • NULL values will be returned if a condition doesn’t exist in a connect restriction. 
  • The input parameters can be omitted as they are supplemented by default values. 

Authentication/Connect Failures due to Connect Restrictions 

If a user connection cannot be established due to a connect restriction, you can find details about why the connection was rejected in the “Failed Authentications” page of “User & Authorization Management” tile of SAP HANA Cloud Central. We will talk more about these features in our future blog post.

 

Additional resources to check: 

*User groups support a separation of user management tasks, allowing you to manage related users together and efficiently. A user can belong to only one user group. You can create and manage users in user groups in the SAP HANA Cloud Central and SAP HANA Cockpit. 

More about user groups you can read in SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database Security Guide: User Management -> User Groups, and for more information on how to create, see the SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL Reference Guide: 

  • CREATE USERGROUP Statement (Access Control) 
  • ALTER USERGROUP Statement (Access Control) 

**With the migration of SAP HANA Cockpit functionality in SAP HANA Cloud Central, all of the documentation for these features has also been migrated to the SAP HANA Cloud docu set. 

Moreover, SAP HANA Cloud Central has in-app help that can be accessed by clicking the “?” icon in the shellbar. This in-app help is extensive and contains links to relevant content in the cloud docu set. 

The following links may also be helpful: 

For SAP data lake (SAP HANA Cloud) administration: SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake Administration Guide for Data Lake Relational Engine 

For SAP HANA Cloud administration: SAP HANA Cloud Administration Guide 

For SAP HANA database (SAP HANA Cloud) administration: SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database Administration Guide 

Also, the SAP HANA Cloud Database Administration with SAP HANA Cockpit still exists but will not be updated or maintained.

entry.title: Re: Take Photo in Maintenance Apps on Microsoft Surface G...

Hi Higgo

Microsoft Surface isn't one of the supported platforms unfortunately 

iPad and Android are which is why it works there 

Sorry cant provide better news

 

Best Regards

Enda

As far as I understand, it is much more expensive to get one of this rather than paying again for the package that provides 6 certification attemps.

Thanks.

Alejandro.

entry.title: Re: B2B storefront and SAML SSO integration using okta

entry.title: SAP BW - Master Data Delta Load
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We did a master data enhancement and planning to move the changes to production.

We are using as delta load in BW can someone, please help us on the cut over activities to be perform before moved the changes to ECC Prod.

Do we need to clear the queue?? Like we do in 2LIS_11_VAITM?? 

Thank you for the support in advance.

 

Kind Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Re: Can't change binding for visibility in ui designer
entry.content: Thanks for your response. What does CT stand for?

entry.title: We Need a help to check the punchout catalogues configuration in our S4 system
entry.content:
We need to upload and update the punchout catalogues configuration but we don't have idea on this configuration related punchout catalogues. Specially we are looking into configuration for punchout catalogue.

You can check this in our system by using the transaction code ME23N.in this screen navigate to Catalog and choose catalog. Kindly help with the configuration steps for this particular set up as we need to update the credentials for target catalog in S4 system.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: We Need a help to check the punchout catalogues confi...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

The configuration relating to credentials for a catalog in Materials Management can be found in transaction OLME, then follow the path: Environment Data -> Web Services: ID and Description. This will list all catalogs. 

From here highlight the desired catalog and double click on 'Call structure'. Here the credentials are maintained. 

I hope this helps further. 

Kind Regards, 
Lisa 
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Substitutions in SAP FI
entry.content: @2233667 Attached where?

entry.title: Does API request use SAML assertion like end-user?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

As you know, SAML assertion is used in S/4HANA user login. I'm just wondering if API request (e.g., OData) by communication user also uses SAML and IdP? The other relevant question is that does S/4HANA stores user credentials as well as IdP？

Regards, Eric

entry.title: Re: Interactive Reporting Dashboard for SAP Cloud Integration using SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Sunny-

 

I hope all is well. I am trying to connect CPI running on CF in SAC. I am facing an issue with the connection and it is just throwing a correlation ID error. I have a question. What is the URL which I need to use in the data service? I am using the API endpoint from the integration suite. Please let me know if I am doing something wrong. 

I Appreciate the support.

Regards

HS

entry.title: External candidate profile converted into internal for the wrong employee
entry.content: If a recruiter puts the wrong employee ID on a newly hired candidate profile, and it gets converted to internal for the wrong employee, how can this be corrected?  The wrong employee would see the personal information and application history for the newly hired employee.

entry.title: Re: Outbound IDOC's after migration to S/4 RISE

Have you found any solution to this? We have the same issue now for one of our clients.

Any input is appreciated.

Thanks!
Jyoti

entry.title: 🎓👨🏻‍🎓👩🏻‍🎓📚 Start your professional career in SAP Next-Gen Consulting 💼📈🚀
entry.content:
Undergraduate & Postgraduate Programas by SAP University Alliances North America NA 🇺🇸 🇨🇦

 

| More information Brandenburg University, Germany 🇩🇪 ℹ️ 👇 #education #opportunity #growth #international #university #sap | English (North America) | Spanish (Latin America) | Portuguese (Brazil) |🇺🇸 🇨🇦 |🇲🇽 | 🇧🇷|

Dear SAP University Alliances North America Members,

Great news! We have launched this year's new offer special for you. 🎉

Since Brandenburg University Germany 🇩🇪 has the new SAP UCC CHICO 🇺🇸 academic alliance cooperation 🤝 for North America (NA), all its members will have a special discount on our IT Certification programs this Spring 2024. 🎓📚

  • 20% Member Universities SAP UCC CHICO. List HERE
  • 10% SAP University Alliances member universities in North America. List HERE

The registration for the next SAP online programs (Spring Term 2024) started!

NEWS: In Spring Term 2024 we are launching the new topic SAP Materials Management (SAP S/4HANA MM). For more details, please visit our website: www.erp4students.org

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In Spring Term 2024 erp4students offers students 17 certification programs for following topics:

  • Enterprise Resource Planning with SAP S/4HANA (TS410)
  • SAP Financial Accounting with SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Controlling with SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Materials Management (SAP S/4HANA MM) NEW!!!
  • SAP Sales & Distribution with SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Production Planning and Manufacturing
  • SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) NEW!!!
  • SAP Business Warehouse
  • SAP S/4HANA Customizing
  • ABAP Programming

The programs will start on 24th May 2024 and end on 22nd September 2024. Registration deadline is 21st May 2024.

This program offers complete flexibility to fit your busy life. Learn from anywhere with an internet connection, on your schedule –  weekdays, weekends, even during semester breaks. All course materials are accessible 24/7, so you can study at your own pace.

Need a hand? Our dedicated tutors are just an email away and provide prompt support, even outside of traditional business hours. This personalized guidance ensures you get the most out of your learning experience, regardless of your schedule.

Includes:

  • University Certificate with 6 ECTS and Grade valid in North America
  • Option of Credit Transfer to your University
  • SAP-Certification

For the Exclusive 10% & 20% Discount Codes: [registration-es@erp4students.org]

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Following the links to our electronic info materials:

  1. The new electronic 2-pager-flyer is here:  https://www.erp4students.com/images/erp4students-com/E-Flyer_COM.pdf
  2. The new detailed electronic brochure is here:  https://www.erp4students.com/images/erp4students-com/Brochure_COM.pdf

If you also need hardcopy flyer and poster, please feel free to contact me.

You find more information on the website for students from North America  https://www.erp4students.com/live/Course-SAP-Registration/registration.aspx

All the best.

-------------------------------------------

Andrés Roa & Pouyan Khatami, Dipl. Wirt.-Inf.

Erp4students | TH Brandenburg

Regional Managing Co-Director NA LAC BRA

Email: andres.roa@erp4students.org    

https://www.erp4students.org/

#NewOffer #SAPUCCCHICO #BrandenburgUniversity #EpistemyPress #SAPUA #Partnership

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Christian Klein | Pouyan Khatami | Tom Wilder | Jeff Word | Guy Couillard | Heather (Czech) Matthews | Colleen Raftery |

entry.title: SBPA - How to handle Intermediate Message Event requirement
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have few requirement/scenario to implement using Intermediate Message event in workflow, earlier we  had implement using workflows(Workflow Management).

Now we thought of implementing using complete workflow requirement in Process automation using Business processes, but we don't see Intermediate message event options.

Example scenario : In-between Business Process execution need to wait until get information received from external system (Non - SAP Applications).

Please suggest how to handle this requirements.

Regards,

Satya

entry.title: Resource Management connection with Collaborative Project management
entry.content: How does the task assignment in the Collaborative project Management module links with the staffing of resource requests in the Resource Management module. In other words, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects, how does the Collaborative Project Management link with the Resource Management?

entry.title: Re: Error: Missing text secrets Build iOS
entry.content: Thanks for the update Roberto. Works for me too.

entry.title: Hybris user not authenticated after SAML successful round trip
entry.content:
Hi,

we are using the samlsinglesignon extension to implement SSO with Azure B2C IDP. After going through all the configuration steps, we are able to perform SSO authentication but the hybris user is not authenticated. I was expecting the extension to take care of it.

I extended DefaultSSOService and added the below code:

this.userService.setCurrentUser(user);
this.customerFacade.loginSuccess();
 
In my debugging session, I can see that "user" is the one I authenticated via SSO. However, I see that the "currentUser" is reverted to Anonymous. I can see that when placing a breakpoint in a BeforeControllerHandler code for example.
 
I am not sure what I am missing here

entry.title: Re: how to clean up the change lists after many year usag...
entry.content:
thank you for your good suggestions.

No, I didn't try that as I never worked in HALM archiving functionality. 

Can you please share some ideas on how to use that?

thanks

Kevin

entry.title: Resources displayed to the Resource Manager
entry.content: In the Resource Management module in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects, what resources are displayed to the resource manager? Can he/she see all the company resources? Is it possible to restrict the resource displayed by company or other variable?

entry.title: Differences between VMS vs VSM (Vehicle Sales Management)
entry.content:
Dear experts,

SAP offers VSM solution (Vehicle Sales Management) by the link: 

https://solutionportfolio.net.sap/industry/AUTO/object/6EAE8B28C5D91ED8B4D14907801FC0D2

¿What are the differences similarities? - What is the focus, weakness, strengths (comparatively)

Very thanks.

Lord Lucho

entry.title: Collaborative Project Management task assignment
entry.content: In the Collaborative Project Management in SAP S4HANA Cloud for Projects, can the project manager have access to a planning graphic (Gantt chart or similar) with the resources assigned to the project tasks?

entry.title: How to make different colour for editable KPIs
entry.content:
Hi 

I have two measures both are input fields. I want to apply to colour to one measure (Sales_Input) only and don't want to apply other input measure. I didn't see any option to apply different colour to individual measure.

Any ideas please...

Regards,

Vasu

entry.title: Looking for generic DELVRY03 SHPCON schema
entry.content: Looking for generic DELVRY03 SHPCON schema to map too from EDI in github repo

entry.title: Production order : double confirmation B10 for the same personal number
entry.content:
Hi,

I faced the following problem in CO19: the production operators can confirm twice the same operation with the same personal number and with the same confirmation event B10.

I'm sure it was not the case before we upgraded in EHP8 but I don't have a system EHP7 anymore.

In AFRU, I have 2 lines with B10, same confirmation number, same personal number without a B30 (interruption) in between.

How is it possible ?

Thanks in advance for your support,
Waebou

entry.title: MRP live: Purchasing schedule Lines are not updated by plant run but by materialwise run
entry.content:
All,
we are facing a curious issue. Since a few weeks our system, RISE: Rel 2020, is not planning materials which are set up for use of schedule agreement as relevant for MRP in the daily background jobs. If I run MD01n by particular material, system is planning as expected.
Because of other settings in the material master (use of coverage profile) we are getting warning that the materials can only be planned by classic MRP. We also made seetings in MD_MRP_FORCE_CLASSIC ("force classic" and "accept issue") but no effect.

Any ideas or suggestions

entry.title: Re: Testing SAP Build Process Automation task UI in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
@MioYasutake 

 

Scratch that. I got Northwind to work.. 

Hopefully, this gets me closer to getting my Odata service to work.

entry.title: Re: Setup SAP Task Center with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
@Pavithra_M , the behavior is not normal and I would expect you should see the task details without getting a login prompt.  I haven't looked at this setup for some time so not sure if something changed in the setup process.  Can you reach out to me directly.

Thanks, Harjeet Judge

entry.title: Re: Output for STO Goods Issue
entry.content:
641 is WL Transaction/Event type. Not WA or WE. With WL for me it works. 

I think this was the issue.

entry.title: Can I apply WBS elements at item level on service orders using migration cockpit?
entry.content:
Hello, 

 

I am currently working on sap Public cloud. I would want to know if it is possible to add WBS elements at item level on service orders using migration cockpit. 

As I can understand, this object is not available on the standard cockpit and I think we can upload a template with the Mapping of Work Breakdown Structure Element (WBS Element) but when I apply it and check for updates on the template, there is no change or new zone dedicated for WBS elements. there is any document explaining that process? 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Substitutions in SAP FI
entry.content:
Please find the attachment

 

 

Form Routine pool Program : YGRFXX595

Exit is NS23 , definition in exit titles as below

No Rules are defined

 

 

 

 

 

 

Form Routine

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

T80D table entry as below

2nd row

 

 

Obbh screen shot

 

 

 

 

Its giving the error ‘NS22’ not found

 

 

 

No rules are defined.

entry.title: Save File using File Dialog
smarttable barcode
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to convert a smart table cell into a barcode image.

I am using the function JsBarcode inside a formatter but I am having an error because, I think, the table is not rendered yet.

In my view.xml I have:

 

                            <cells>
                                 <Image id="T" src="{path: 'Obj_key', formatter:'.formatBarcode'}" />
                            </cells>
 
Is there a way I can manipulate a smart table item cell after it has been rendered, as I think this might solve the issue?.
Thanks,
Javier

entry.title: Re: Save File using File Dialog
entry.content: The task is to enable a file storing from cockpit application. The bytes should be stored on client computer, at best by using a file dialog. That file should be used later by the customer

entry.title: External users/ native sign in users password reset in LMS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is there any way to reset the External users password without going in to LMS system?

When users are trying to use the Forgot password option in External LMS site, they are being asked for security question answer but most of the time users are unable to remember the answer for security question.

so, in that case only admin can reset the password...i want to know Is there any other way to reset the external users password without going into LMS system.

Thanks

entry.title: Single Inbound delivery with multiple containers
entry.content:
Hi, gurus.

I'm new to S/4 Hana and EWM and I'm working as a material planner.

We are building a new system with consultants and the IT department but there is a problem with the management of inbound delivery.

I have a material that the monthly requirement is about 10,000 tons.

Delivery lead-time is about 2 months and B/L is usually issued with hundreds of tons of the material.

But the point of view for management way is different with IT department.

They are trying to make inbound deliveries for each container because they need to trace the materials each containers have.

In this case, about 1,000 inbound deliveries covering 2 months of the lead time will remain every time and I should manage the inbound deliveries. (a container is 20t / 20,000t will be goods in transit to cover the lead time)

We even do not use EDI and will upload an Excel sheet for creating inbound delivery.

I think that the inbound delivery should be created based on B/L and it should be managed by handling unit or another way.

Would you please give a tip for this situation?

I'd like to know if I can use the HU identification 2 field for distinguishing containers.

 

- Requirements

1) Top-level Handling Unit is a pallet.

 -> I suggested they use a container as one of HU but it has been denied due to time limitation.

2) They want to post GR based on a container.

3) They also want to know what pallet is in which container.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Lock object (SM12) from UI5 Application
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

I've been searching for this, optimistic, pessimistic, durable locks, etc .. 
But still couldn't achieve what I'm looking for. 

I've tried using a Function Import to call the ENQUEUE  function model for a transfer order (LE-WM) but  as soon the call ends the lock is cleared in SM12. I know and understand the issues that may arise ( close browser, etc .. ) but that's not a problem at all for the whole solution. I want to be able to lock the object and it should stay in SM12 until I call the DEQUEUE function model. 
Any tips are welcome, thanks in advance 🙂

entry.content:
Error when opening Learning Center

 

 

we have maintained a value for the parameter LEARNING_APP_BACKEND_URL to be the same as the value for maintained parameter DATA_URL_WPB2

but it still fails

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Integration scenarios with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system
entry.content:
Thanks, Muthu for sharing.

Item 1 (in the above table) works with and without SLP; i.e. If the customer has SAP Ariba SLP entitlement, the integration can be bi-directional else unidirectional.

entry.title: question, is there any medical management module integrated to SAP Employee central or SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hello experts, we would like to know if there is a module where you can manage medical appointments, medication control, etc. at medical office level for an industry.
Not an independent medical center or hospital, but a small internal clinic within an industry.

Is there any solution that integrates to EMPLOYEE CENTRAL or any application already made in sap BTP or SAP independent module.

I would appreciate your comments

Thanks for everything.

entry.title: Re: Testing SAP Build Process Automation task UI in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content: For those following along. My Destination for my Odata service wasn't set up correctly. It didn't have the correct properties added and the URL was missing the Port number.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: IDM 7.2 - FromSAP-Parameter FILTER - Question
entry.content: Hi JaiSuryan, Ranjith,

entry.title: Re: Clean Up Unused Salesforce Input Key Figures - SAP IBP
entry.content: We have only 2 Custom Planning area.

entry.title: Re: IDM 7.2 - FromSAP-Parameter FILTER - Question
entry.content: Hi JaiSuryan, Ranjith, Where can I find this SELECTION query documentation how to use it etc. Please guide. Regards laxmi

entry.title: Re: IM, WM and EWM - selecting the right solution
entry.content:
@christina_wengI'm sure you're not still waiting in 2024 for an answer to the question posted in 2019 as a comment to 2013 blog post. But this post ranks high in Google search when looking for the answer on differences between IM, WM, EWM, so I thought I'd comment in case anyone else thinks along the same lines.

Assuming by PS module you meant Project Services, I'm scratching my head what do you think would be the relationship between it and any warehouse functionality... I hope you're not stockpiling people in the warehouse. But if a project also uses some materials in addition to human resources, then obviously any warehouse functionality can be applied to those materials as well.

If I may say, the way of looking at the "modules" and asking is something "compatible" seems like a fundamentally wrong way to look at SAP ERP. It's not as much about the modules as it is about the processes. PS is not isolated from other modules. For your projects, you'll likely also utilize sales documents and all the material-related functionality that comes with it. This part won't be different from any other modules / processes.

By the way, if anyone also stumbled upon this post in 2024 or later, WM is going away in 2025, IM is now called "Stock Room Management" (or something like that) and only EWM will be supported going forward. Unfortunately, in the recent migration SAP Community got completely borked and I've not bee able to find easily any posts here with the summary similar to this 2013 post. The best post from third parties I found is this one: https://blog.shiperp.com/sap-wm-vs-sap-ewm

Cheers!

entry.title: Re: XML to JSON Converter
entry.content:
I have this XML as output from an RFC FM

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?>
<rfc:Z_CUSTOMER_BALANCE.Response xmlns:rfc="urn:sap-com:document:sap:rfc:functions">
	<ET_CUSTOMERS_BALANCE>
		<item>
			<CUSTOMER>0001000011</CUSTOMER>
			<CUSTOMER_NAME>ELIAS KEKAKOS</CUSTOMER_NAME>
			<CURRENCY>EUR</CURRENCY>
			<BALANCE>39640.8200</BALANCE>
			<MESSAGE/>
		</item>
	</ET_CUSTOMERS_BALANCE>
	<E_BALANCE>39640.8200</E_BALANCE>
	<E_CURRENCY>EUR</E_CURRENCY>
	<E_CUSTNAME>ELIAS KEKAKOS</E_CUSTNAME>
	<E_CUSTOMER>0001000011</E_CUSTOMER>
	<E_RETURN_MESSAGE/>
</rfc:Z_CUSTOMER_BALANCE.Response>

and I am receiving the following JSON

{
    "Z_CUSTOMER_BALANCE.Response": {
        "ET_CUSTOMERS_BALANCE": {
            "item": {
                "CUSTOMER": "0001000011",
                "CUSTOMER_NAME": "ELIAS KEKAKOS",
                "CURRENCY": "EUR",
                "BALANCE": "39640.8200",
                "MESSAGE": ""
            }
        },
        "E_BALANCE": "39640.8200",
        "E_CURRENCY": "EUR",
        "E_CUSTNAME": "ELIAS KEKAKOS",
        "E_CUSTOMER": "0001000011",
        "E_RETURN_MESSAGE": ""
    }
}

I want to have the following output

{
    "Z_CUSTOMER_BALANCE.Response": {
        "ET_CUSTOMERS_BALANCE": {
            "item": [ {
                "CUSTOMER": "0001000011",
                "CUSTOMER_NAME": "ELIAS KEKAKOS",
                "CURRENCY": "EUR",
                "BALANCE": "39640.8200",
                "MESSAGE": ""
            }
           ]
        },
        "E_BALANCE": "39640.8200",
        "E_CURRENCY": "EUR",
        "E_CUSTNAME": "ELIAS KEKAKOS",
        "E_CUSTOMER": "0001000011",
        "E_RETURN_MESSAGE": ""
    }
}

But when I enable STREAMIMG and put in XML ELEMENT the following  Z_CUSTOMER_BALANCE.Response/ET_CUSTOMERS_BALANCE/item nothing is happening and if I have more than 1 ITEMS they appeared as structures like

{
    "Z_CUSTOMER_BALANCE.Response": {
        "ET_CUSTOMERS_BALANCE": {
            "item": {
                "CUSTOMER": "0001000022",
                "CUSTOMER_NAME": "ELIAS KEKAKOS",
                "CURRENCY": "EUR",
                "BALANCE": "238.6900",
                "MESSAGE": ""
            },
            "item": {
                "CUSTOMER": "0001000035",
                "CUSTOMER_NAME": "ELIAS KEKAKOS2",
                "CURRENCY": "EUR",
                "BALANCE": "20639.1000",
                "MESSAGE": ""
            }
      },
        "E_BALANCE": "0.0000",
        "E_CURRENCY": "",
        "E_CUSTNAME": "",
        "E_CUSTOMER": "",
        "E_RETURN_MESSAGE": ""
    }
}

Is there any explanation for this?

Thanks in advance

Elias

entry.title: Re: Redwood RMJ Architecture, Integration and Interface review with SAP S/4 HANA and ECC Job executi
entry.content: Very nice blog! Thanks for the detail explanation

entry.title: sapui5 smarttable barcode
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to convert a smart table cell into a barcode image.

I am using the function JsBarcode inside a formatter but I am having an error because, I think, the table is not rendered yet.

In my view.xml I have:

 

                            <cells>
                                 <Image id="T" src="{path: 'Obj_key', formatter:'.formatBarcode'}" />
                            </cells>
 
Is there a way I can manipulate a smart table item cell after it has been rendered, as I think this might solve the issue?.
Thanks,
Javier

entry.title: Re: ABAP Matrix (AFM/ALM)
entry.content:
This is a great initiative! We've recently had an internal discussion at work about the base knowledge that every ABAPer should have. Because of so many changes in recent years and working with clients on very different releases, it's important to know this type of feature availability. However, this raised a valid question: how is anyone supposed to know that? As you correctly noted, currently this information is scattered.

While we were aiming at more general knowledge (e.g. "stop using classic CDS views in S/4HANA 2020" and such), having clear and detailed matrix would be very helpful.

If I may suggest, would it make more sense to arrange columns in chronological order from left to right? I looked at the table first and thought "wait, what? pooled tables are back now?" 🙂 but then realized it's in a different order (newer to the left). I believe left to right is more natural way to present this. At least that's what I've seen in similar matrices.

Thanks for sharing this!

entry.title: Re: Not able to create any decision object using SAP Buil...
entry.content: Hello, Issue is resolved. Kindly check now

entry.title: Extract Data from File fetched from UI
entry.content:
Hi All,

From UI I can able to fetch the file and store it into a folder but in next step I'm facing little issue to fetch file from folder.
Using From ASCII pass I can achieve it but I need to make it dynamic so that in next action task it takes the file from same place where it had store in first action task.
I tried storing the path in job variable in action task 1 but couldn't able to fetch this variable data in action task 2.

Please assist.

Thanks & Regards,
Laxmi
